name: Lexaria Bioscience Corp.
business: Lexaria Bioscience Corp. is a biotechnology company that licenses its patented DehydraTECH drug delivery technology to enhance the bioavailability of active pharmaceutical ingredients across multiple ingestible product formats. The company generates revenue through technology licensing agreements and B2B processing of DehydraTECH-enhanced intermediary products, primarily serving pharmaceutical, nutraceutical, and consumer packaged goods sectors.

exposures.sentence: Lexaria Bioscience Corp. is a biotechnology company dedicated to the enhancement of the bioavailability of a diverse and broad range of active pharmaceutical ingredients ( APIs ) using our patented DehydraTECH TM drug delivery technology.
During fiscal 2025 Lexaria marked significant milestones in further investigating and developing DehydraTECH-processed GLP-1 and GIP formulations for the treatment of diabetes and weight loss.

Our current patent portfolio includes patent family applications or grants pertaining to Lexaria s compositions, methods of use in improving API bioavailability and palatability and methods of treatment for a range of therapeutic indications, for a wide variety of APIs encompassing ... cannabinoids.
Lexaria Nicotine LLC, (16.667% owned by Altria Ventures Inc.) holds the exclusive rights to the use or sublicense of DehydraTECH with nicotine molecules.
Lexaria is determined to fill the need for a safe, effective, tolerable treatment for hypertension and have a meaningful impact on comorbidity-related costs and deaths with our DehydraTECH-CBD.

catalysts.event: Last patient last visit achieved in chronic human study GLP-1-H24-4
catalysts.sentence: On August 14, 2025, the Company announced that the important study milestone known as last patient last visit had been achieved in this study, such that full sample and data analyses could then be undertaken pursuant to the late calendar-2025 final reporting objective.

catalysts.event: Interim 8-week results from chronic human study GLP-1-H24-4 released
catalysts.sentence: On July 28, 2025, preliminary results at the 8-week interim point of the study were released.

catalysts.event: Human pilot study GLP-1-H25-5 partial results announced
catalysts.sentence: The partial results as announced on June 11, 2025 evidenced that orally delivered DehydraTECH-liraglutide produced fewer adverse events as compared to injected Saxenda while having comparable measurements in blood glucose, insulin and body weight-control.

As announced on September 19, 2025, the study results from ex vivo organ imaging revealed an interesting trend whereby, when tested against the na ve and vehicle groups, the DehydraTECH FTS composition demonstrated a predominantly higher apparent trend in brain biodistribution as compared to the Rybelsus mimicking formulation.

catalysts.event: FDA IND submission for HYPER-H23-1 and Study May Proceed letter
catalysts.sentence: On January 29, 2024, Lexaria submitted its IND application with the FDA and it received a Study May Proceed letter from the FDA on February 29, 2024.

catalysts.event: Completion of animal study WEIGHT-A24-1 weight-control results
catalysts.sentence: On November 20, 2024, Lexaria published the 12-week weight-control performance and blood sugar control performance results for all study arms.

catalysts.event: Human pilot study GLP-1-H24-3 results announced
catalysts.sentence: The results as announced on January 14, 2025 and March 18, 2025, evidenced that orally delivered DehydraTECH-tirzepatide produced fewer adverse events as compared to injected Zepbound

tamClaims.claim: Anti-obesity market in 2030 will be $100 billion, with $25 billion for oral market share
tamClaims.sentence: Published reports indicate that the market size just for the anti-obesity market in 2030 will be $100 billion of which $25 billion has been projected for the oral market share.

tamClaims.claim: Treatment-resistant hypertension market valued at $43 million in 2023, expected to reach $159.4 million by 2033
tamClaims.sentence: Treatment-resistant hypertension, valued at $43 million in 2023 and expected to reach $159.4 million by 2033 (https://www.futuremarketinsights.com/reports/treatment-resistant-hypertension-management-market).

tamClaims.claim: Diabetes medical costs and lost work/wages total $413 billion yearly
tamClaims.sentence: Medical costs and lost work and wages for people with diagnosed diabetes total $413 billion yearly.

tamClaims.claim: Obesity-related costs to US healthcare system reach almost $173 billion a year
tamClaims.sentence: And that 1 in 5 children and 2 in 5 adults have obesity, which can result in numerous health conditions, including high blood pressure, heart disease and type 2 diabetes with costs to the US healthcare system reaching almost $173 billion a year.
